discovery.run_summary: Risk-on tape with software/AI names ripping (Snowflake, Dell, Okta, MSFT, ORCL) and defense names surging on apparent Iran tension headlines. Standout idiosyncratic event is PLAB collapsing on earnings with multi-day pre-print weakness, while defense complex (AVAV, KTOS, MRCY) extends gains alongside ITA strength.

notable_index_moves: XLK +1.3%, SMH +0.7%, MSFT +3.5%, ORCL +6.7% — software/AI leadership on Snowflake/Dell/Okta prints
ITA +2.2% with defense mid-caps (AVAV, KTOS, MRCY) outperforming on Iran missile headlines
XLU -1.1% and XLF -0.3% as defensive/rate-sensitive names lag risk-on rotation

discoveries.name: Photronics, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Earnings reaction with very heavy volume and pre-print weakness already baked in
discoveries.thesis: An ~36% single-day collapse on 8x volume following a 2.02 earnings 8-K, in a small semi-equipment supplier, is the signature of a genuine miss + guide cut, not an overreaction. The fact that the stock was already down ~32% over five days suggests informed selling preceded the print — the magnitude of the gap is consistent with a structural reset (guide cut, customer loss, or margin collapse) rather than a one-quarter blip. Hard to call OVERDONE without seeing the actual numbers; in photomask, customer concentration risk (large foundry/IDM orders) means a single lost program can reset the multiple permanently.
discoveries.what_confirms: 8-K exhibits showing FY guide cut, gross margin compression, or specific commentary on lost share at a major foundry customer; analyst downgrades with new price targets clustered near current price
discoveries.what_kills: If the print shows only a modest miss with no guide change and the move is driven by a single forced seller or panic — then a snapback toward the pre-drawdown level becomes likely and OVERDONE would have been the right call
discoveries.what_to_learn: When a stock has already bled 25%+ into an earnings print on rising volume, the post-print gap is usually the confirmation of a real fundamental break, not a fadeable overreaction — informed selling rarely leads a clean beat
discoveries.catalyst: Q2 earnings release (8-K filed 2026-05-28 with items 2.02 + 9.01)
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: 2.02 earnings 8-K filed same day as a 36% drop on 8x volume, preceded by 5-day decline of ~32% — pattern of informed selling into a confirmed bad print
research_pointers: Read the 8-K exhibit for FY26 guidance and specifically commentary on Asia (Taiwan/Korea) photomask demand
Check whether a major customer (Samsung, TSMC, or memory IDM) signaled an order push-out
Compare gross margin trajectory vs prior 4 quarters — photomask margins are operationally levered
Check options skew and short interest — if SI was already elevated, a squeeze on any positive update is possible

discoveries.name: AeroVironment, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Geopolitical-event sympathy rally in a defense mid-cap extending into a 30%+ five-day run
discoveries.thesis: AVAV up ~18% today on 3.3x volume with no 8-K, riding Iran missile headlines and broader defense tape (ITA +2.2%). The stock is up ~31% in five days with no company-specific catalyst visible — that's a positioning move, not a fundamentals move. Drone/loitering-munition names get bid hard on Middle East escalation, but historically these spikes retrace 30-50% within two weeks once the headline news fades, absent an actual contract award. The five-day magnitude is now disproportionate to what's been confirmed.
discoveries.what_confirms: Headlines stabilize on Iran (ceasefire holds), no fresh DoD contract award lands in next 5 trading days, AVAV gives back a meaningful portion of the five-day gain
discoveries.what_kills: An actual large Switchblade or JUMP-20 contract announcement, or material Iran escalation (strikes, Strait closure) — either would justify the move and convert it to UNDERDONE
discoveries.what_to_learn: Defense mid-caps that gap 15%+ on a single geopolitical news day without an 8-K or contract release tend to fade unless the headline keeps escalating; the five-day return is the better read than the one-day for fade setups
discoveries.catalyst: Iran missile launch headlines and sector-wide defense bid (ITA +2.2%)
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals — no 8-K, no earnings; news flow shows Iran missile launches as the dominant geopolitical headline driving defense tape
research_pointers: Check DoD contract announcements over the past week for AVAV specifically
Look at AVAV's last earnings call commentary on Switchblade backlog vs. consensus expectations
Compare AVAV's five-day return vs. KTOS and MRCY — relative outperformance often signals retail/momentum positioning
Monitor Iran ceasefire reporting; CNBC noted prediction-market chances of a deal did NOT improve

discoveries.name: Watsco, Inc.
discoveries.setup: HVAC distributor down on no visible catalyst in a risk-on tape
discoveries.thesis: WSO down ~4% in a tape where SPY is +0.55% and with no 8-K — but XLU -1.1% and rate-sensitive/housing-exposed names appear to be the losers today. Could be tied to housing/HVAC demand concerns or a sell-side note we can't see. Without a visible catalyst, can't articulate either bull (oversold/buy-the-dip) or bear (fundamental break) at conf 3+.
discoveries.what_confirms: A sell-side downgrade or channel check note, or weak HVAC distributor commentary from a peer (e.g., FERG, POOL action)
discoveries.what_kills: Confirms it's just sector rotation and snaps back tomorrow
discoveries.catalyst: Unknown — possible HVAC/housing demand concern or peer-driven rotation
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals; defensive industrial distributor weak against a risk-on tape with XLI -0.3% and XLU -1.1%
research_pointers: Check for any morning sell-side note on HVAC distribution or residential reno demand
Compare to FERG, POOL, SITE for sector-wide read
Look at recent weekly distributor commentary on R-454B/R-32 transition inventory dynamics

discoveries.name: Kratos Defense & Security Solutions
discoveries.setup: Defense mid-cap bid on Iran headlines with sector tailwind
discoveries.thesis: Up ~14% on 2.5x volume, five-day +17% — strong but less extended than AVAV. Kratos has genuine ongoing CCA/drone program newsflow and the Iran tape provides a coherent reason. Magnitude is sharp but not yet at the extension level where a fade is high-conviction. Watch for whether this is following AVAV's setup or has its own contract catalyst.
discoveries.what_confirms: A specific CCA or target-drone contract release in next few days would shift this to UNDERDONE
discoveries.what_kills: If five-day pushes past 25%+ without contract news, fade setup becomes much more attractive
discoveries.catalyst: Defense sector bid on Iran missile headlines
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: No 8-K; sector-wide defense move with ITA +2.2% and peer AVAV +18%
research_pointers: Check for any Valkyrie/CCA program updates this week
Compare KTOS extension to AVAV — if KTOS catches up to AVAV's five-day move without news, that's a fade signal

catalyst_chains.primary_event: Iran missile launches and Trump deal/ceasefire ambiguity driving defense bid
Snowflake +36% / Dell +23% / Okta +8% earnings beats driving software/AI rally

trump_signals.post_summary: Trump reaffirms commitment to crypto-friendly regulation and 'future-proof' digital asset market structure
trump_signals.note: Reiteration of existing pro-crypto stance — incremental, not new policy. Unlikely to drive a fresh leg in crypto-adjacent equities by itself, but worth noting if crypto legislation calendar updates emerge.

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: The biggest movers in the market data do NOT map cleanly to today's AI announcements. PLAB -36% is an earnings miss (8-K item 2.02 filed today) — semiconductor photomask, not AI-driven. AVAV +18%, KTOS +14%, MRCY +11% are a defense/drone rally (likely geopolitical or budget catalyst, not AI news in this feed). Healthcare movers (RGEN, VCYT, ALGN, MEDP, CRSP) and FIVE/AMR/WSO have no plausible link to the listed AI announcements. Anthropic Opus 4.8 + Dynamic Workflows would normally pressure workflow-automation SaaS names (MNDY, ASAN, NOW, PEGA, U) and coding-tool incumbents, but none are in the provided moves list. Quiet night for AI-attributable equity dislocations in this dataset. Flagging that two of today's top stories are Anthropic-originated — bias risk acknowledged, but no actionable Anthropic-vs-public-stock call to make from the data given.
